meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills Setup: Debugging Guide for Students
content.seo_description: Master Claude Code and Antigravity to solve complex debugging issues. This guide helps students bridge the gap between theory and production-ready code.
content.answer_block: To overcome debugging gridlock, integrate Claude Code directly into your terminal workflow. By leveraging AI-driven context analysis, you can identify logic errors, trace stack overflows, and refactor brittle code in seconds. Combining this with Antigravity’s structural insights allows you to automate the resolution process, turning frustrating bugs into clear learning milestones.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal full of red stack traces, the cursor blinking like a countdown clock to a deadline you’re not sure you’ll meet. The code that worked yesterday is now a black box of cryptic errors, and the frustration of being unable to isolate the root cause is the single greatest barrier to your progress as a developer.

But this isn't a failure—it's the pivot point. By mastering the Claude Code CLI and integrating it with structural analysis tools like Antigravity, you transition from a coder who guesses to an engineer who understands. This setup turns your terminal into a collaborative environment where high-level reasoning meets raw execution.

steps.title: Initialize the Claude Code Environment
steps.description: Configure the Claude Code CLI within your project root to ensure it has full visibility into your file architecture and dependencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the project structure and identify the primary entry point and high-complexity modules.

steps.title: Map the Error Context
steps.description: Use Claude Code to ingest the stack trace and correlate it with relevant files identified by the Antigravity scan.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Map the dependency chain leading to the current runtime exception.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze these logs and map them to the corresponding files in the dependency tree'

steps.title: Isolate the Logic Failure
steps.description: Narrow down the specific function or class causing the crash by requesting a step-by-step trace simulation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mock test case that triggers the identified logic failure in isolation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Explain why this function fails on edge cases and provide a fix'

steps.title: Automated Remediation
steps.description: Apply a surgical fix using Claude's ability to rewrite code blocks while maintaining existing interface contracts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a refactor that preserves current function signatures while resolving the runtime error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix --file <filename> --prompt 'Apply the suggested refactor to fix the logic error'

steps.title: Verification and Regression Testing
steps.description: Validate that the fix is robust and does not introduce new issues in related modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Check if this change creates any downstream side effects in the project architecture.

pro_tips: Always use the '--verbose' flag in Claude Code when debugging obscure memory leaks to see the reasoning chain.
Treat Antigravity as a structural map; if it shows a circular dependency, fix that before asking Claude to debug logic.
Commit your code after every successful Claude-assisted fix to create a granular git history for educational review.
Use Claude's '/compact' command if your context window becomes cluttered with irrelevant system logs.
Request 'Explain the error in the context of design patterns' to level up your architectural knowledge while fixing the bug.
content.next_steps: Once your debugging workflow is automated, begin building a 'Bug Journal' where you document the top 5 most common errors you encounter and the Claude Code commands used to solve them.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical edge to turn debugging sessions into deep-learning opportunities. Don't let silent failures stall your momentum. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
